admissibility_rule.A1_authoritative_source: authoritative source (official HF livecodebench/* dataset OR the official LiveCodeBench GitHub repo; NOT an aggregator / mirror / leaderboard-site intro / blog rumor / 'planned' announcement)
admissibility_rule.A2_dated_problems: dated problems (each carries a contest_date time-anchor)
admissibility_rule.A3_functional_compatible: functional/code-generation-compatible (has a starter_code FUNCTIONAL subset the W89 mechanism can attack)
admissibility_rule.A4_sha_pinnable_admittable: machine-checkable provenance (a SHA-256-pinnable JSONL artifact that can be operator-fetched + pinned + admitted to LIVECODEBENCH_KNOWN_RELEASES)
admissibility_rule.A5_reproducible_histogram: reproducible date histogram (the functional month histogram can be re-derived from the pinned bytes)

instrument_admissibility.label: release_v6 (admitted instrument)
instrument_admissibility.reason: ADMISSIBLE_BUT_NOT_NEWER: release_v6 (admitted instrument) is A1..A5-admissible but does not post-date the admitted instrument (version<= 6 and frontier<= 2025-04-05). The current admitted instrument: admissible but NOT new (functional frontier 2025-04-05, already the certification base).

instrument_admissibility.label: full code_generation dataset
instrument_admissibility.reason: ADMISSIBLE_BUT_NOT_NEWER: full code_generation dataset is A1..A5-admissible but does not post-date the admitted instrument (version<= 6 and frontier<= 2025-04-05). The full (non-lite) dataset: authoritative + dated + functional, but its README frontier is the SAME Apr-2025 => admissible-as-source but NOT newer than the admitted lite v6.

instrument_admissibility.label: release_latest alias
instrument_admissibility.reason: ADMISSIBLE_BUT_NOT_NEWER: release_latest alias is A1..A5-admissible but does not post-date the admitted instrument (version<= 6 and frontier<= 2025-04-05). The upstream loader's 'latest' alias resolves to the SAME six files as release_v6 => no hidden newer-than-v6 supply.

instrument_admissibility.label: planned release_v7 (search rumor)
instrument_admissibility.reason: NOT_ADMISSIBLE [A1_authoritative,A2_dated,A3_functional,A4_sha_pinnable,A5_histogram fail]: A 'planned v7' mentioned only by a non-primary summary; contradicted by all four authoritative surfaces; no artifact, no SHA => REFUSED by A1 + A4. Recorded as the W117 watch signal.

notes: no upstream change vs baseline (frontier unchanged)

instrument.note: LCB code_generation_lite release_v6 (HF file tree: test6.jsonl is the highest-numbered => latest; no test7+ as of 2026-05-29). Full release May-2023..Apr-2025; the FUNCTIONAL/lite subset attacked by the W89 mechanism is 63 problems, all 2025-01..04. Instrument frontier date 2025-04-05.

per_model.reason: NOT_CERTIFIABLE_FOR_NEW_PILOT [C4 already settled]: W113 resistant pilot on release_v6 (CID 2afc318c): B-A1=+0.00pp FAIL => EXPOSURE_CONFIRMED. Settled cell. A second pilot on the same instrument has no verdict-changing power (redundant).

per_model.primary_source: Official HF model card (Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-480B-A35B-Instruct) + Qwen blog (qwenlm.github.io/blog/qwen3-coder)
per_model.reason: NOT_CERTIFIABLE [C1 cutoff UNKNOWN]: cannot certify resistance against a non-KNOWN cutoff. Official HF model card (Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-480B-A35B-Instruct) + Qwen blog (qwenlm.github.io/blog/qwen3-coder) => UNKNOWN. Both primary sources state NO knowledge/data cutoff ('NO CUTOFF STATED'). Released 2025-07; an estimable cutoff would be ~2025, at/after the Apr-2025 functional frontier => UNKNOWN and almost certainly C2-exposed even if disclosed.

per_model.primary_source: DeepSeek official sources (no V4 cutoff disclosed; V3 system-prompt extraction ~Jul-2024 is non-official)
per_model.reason: NOT_CERTIFIABLE [C1 cutoff UNKNOWN]: cannot certify resistance against a non-KNOWN cutoff. DeepSeek official sources (no V4 cutoff disclosed; V3 system-prompt extraction ~Jul-2024 is non-official) => UNKNOWN. No official V4 training cutoff published. V4 post-dates V3 (2024-12), so an estimable cutoff is >= early-2025 => UNKNOWN.

NOT_CERTIFIABLE [C1 cutoff UNKNOWN]: cannot certify resistance against a non-KNOWN cutoff. Official Mistral docs/model card (mistral-small-4-0-26-03) + HF (mistralai/Mistral-Small-4-119B-2603) => UNKNOWN. No training cutoff stated. Released 2026-03-16, which post-dates the ENTIRE release_v6 window => exposed even if a cutoff were disclosed => UNKNOWN.

decision.w115_blocker: The latest resistant FUNCTIONAL instrument (release_v6; functional 2025-01-11..2025-04-05) does not post-date a single reachable stronger-than-Maverick model's VERIFIABLE cutoff. A >= 30 functional resistant slice requires a KNOWN cutoff <= ~2025-01; the reachable frontier models (Qwen3-Coder-480B 2025-07, DeepSeek-V4-pro 2025+, Mistral-Small-4 2026-03) have OFFICIALLY UNDISCLOSED cutoffs (C1) and, where estimable, cutoffs at/after the Apr-2025 frontier (C2) — the gaps COMPOUND. Maverick is the only KNOWN cutoff and is already SETTLED here (W113). => NO new stronger-model resistant pilot is certifiable on the latest real data.
decision.next_instrument_requirement: A resistant FUNCTIONAL instrument with >= 30 problems dated strictly after a reachable frontier model's KNOWN cutoff. Concretely: (a) a future LCB release_v7+ (or equivalent freshly-dated functional benchmark) with post-2025-04 functional problems, operator-fetched + SHA-pinned AND admitted to the loader; AND (b) a reachable stronger-than-Maverick model whose cutoff is officially DISCLOSED (KNOWN) and earlier than those problems. Neither holds as of 2026-05-29.

w116_fire_condition.instrument_trigger: A newer official LCB release (release_v7+) is observed on the source, operator-fetched + SHA-pinned + admitted to LIVECODEBENCH_KNOWN_RELEASES, with >= 30 functional problems dated strictly after a reachable stronger-than-Maverick model's KNOWN cutoff.
w116_fire_condition.cutoff_trigger: A reachable stronger-than-Maverick model discloses a KNOWN cutoff month <= 2025-01 (so the current release_v6 admits a >= 30 resistant slice for it); update MODEL_TRAINING_CUTOFFS + W114_CUTOFF_PROVENANCE, then re-run.

w117_fire_condition.instrument_trigger: An admissible NEW upstream instrument (a release_v7+ on the lite tree, OR release_latest re-pointing past release_v6, OR a distinct upstream functional dataset with post-2025-04 dated problems) is observed, operator-fetched + SHA-pinned + admitted to LIVECODEBENCH_KNOWN_RELEASES, with >= 30 functional problems dated strictly after a reachable stronger-than-Maverick model's primary-KNOWN cutoff (RUNBOOK § 3 + § 5).
w117_fire_condition.cutoff_trigger: A reachable stronger-than-Maverick model discloses, from a PRIMARY source, a KNOWN cutoff month <= 2025-01 (so the current release_v6 admits a >= 30 resistant slice for it); update MODEL_TRAINING_CUTOFFS + W114_CUTOFF_PROVENANCE, then re-run.
